description_long: This stereo sound system features a rectangular, boxy enclosure with a matte black finish. The front face displays two prominent circular speaker elements, with the lower one being larger and likely serving as the woofer, while the upper one is a smaller tweeter, both protected by a perforated grille. The rear side includes a recessed control panel with visible knobs and ports, as well as a circular port for bass reflex. The sides and top are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the minimalist design. The overall construction appears robust, likely made from dense materials to reduce vibration and enhance sound quality. This object is suitable for placement in urban environments such as garages, home studios, or public event spaces.
description: A matte black, box-shaped stereo sound system with two circular speakers on the front and a control panel with ports on the back. The design is minimalist and robust, suitable for urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing two circular speaker drivers, one large and one small, with a perforated grille.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a smooth, featureless black surface with no visible controls or openings.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a control panel with knobs and ports, as well as a circular bass reflex port.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, also smooth and featureless, matching the other side.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grilles have a circular perforated pattern with evenly spaced small holes, forming a mesh-like protective cover over the drivers.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is located on the rear side, featuring several round knobs and ports arranged vertically within a rectangular recessed area.
